verdict_reasoning: CCR8 is a canonical C-C chemokine receptor and 7-transmembrane G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) with extracellular loops and N-terminus forming the chemokine-binding site. Assigned CD nomenclature (CDw198) and registry-curated membership in the C-C chemokine receptor family. The protein's function — binding circulating chemokines (I-309, TARC, MIP-1β) to regulate immune-cell migration — requires surface accessibility; this is a prototypical cell-surface receptor.
